message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Pinzimini (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 256 reviews; OpenTable 1,140+ diner ratings; TripAdvisor listing active. Combined cross-platform ~1,500-2,000 [Google count UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: mostly positive — service consistently praised, food rated good but uneven across reviews; OpenTable 3.7 stars suggests mixed-to-positive rather than top-tier
- Longevity: Opened 2012 per discovery sources; ~14 years operating under The Westin Rancho Mirage Golf Resort & Spa (Marriott/Westin brand)
- Credentials: ["Executive Chef Joel Delmond referenced as 'James Beard Honored' on restaurant's own site [UNVERIFIED — specific Beard recognition not confirmed via independent source]","Listed on USA Today 10Best Rancho Mirage restaurants"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces in 'best breakfast Rancho Mirage' (brunch buffet mentions), 'Italian restaurants Rancho Mirage' (mid-list, not top), and resort-dining queries tied to Westin. Does NOT surface in 'best Italian Coachella Valley' (Norma's, Enzo's, Mario's dominate) or 'date night Rancho Mirage' (Enzo's, Double Date, Si Bon dominate). Largely invisible in cuisine-first queries — visible only in resort-context queries.
- Site structured data: Own site pinziminipalmsprings.com returns HTTP 403 to automated fetchers (likely bot-protection / CDN block) — agent-hostile. Site name 'pinziminipalmsprings.com' is itself misleading (restaurant is in Rancho Mirage, not Palm Springs). Primary structured presence lives on third-party platforms (OpenTable, Yelp, TripAdvisor, Marriott.com); minimal first-party schema.org reachable.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's a summary of reviews for Pinzimini restaurant at the Westin in Rancho Mirage:

## Overall Rating

Pinzimini is rated 3.7 stars by 1140 OpenTable diners, and has 376 reviews on TripAdvisor with a 4.0 rating.

## Cuisine & Setting

Pinzimini features Italian inspired cuisine, and is located at The Westin Rancho Mirage Golf Resort & Spa, offering coastal-inspired cuisine with fresh, seasonal ingredients and bold flavors in a relaxed, inviting atmosphere.

## Positive Reviews

Strengths consistently mentioned include:
- The restaurant is known for excellent food and attentive service, especially for breakfast with exceptionally good eggs Benedict
- Customer reviews highlight the restaurant's commitment to quality with hand-crafted pasta and flavorful prime rib, with attentive and knowledgeable staff providing expert guidance on menu selections and wine pairings
- Outdoor seating provides a beautiful resort view

## Mixed/Critical Reviews

Some concerns mentioned:
- Some diners found the brunch buffet to be excellent and enjoyed the beautiful environment, while others were disappointed with bland and overpriced dinner options
- Being in the Westin Mission Hills, it's a bit pricey, compared to other local non-resort restaurants
- Some reviews mention pricing being on the steeper side, with occasional comments about variability in dish preparation

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Fine Dining Options

Il Corso offers fine Italian dining with a gorgeous modern design that transports you to a Sicilian villa, with fine wines, Italian-style martinis and decadent dishes. Their grilled lamb chop and wood-fired oven pizzas are standout dishes.

Ristorante Mamma Gina, a historic eatery in Palm Desert since 1986, sits in a prime location within the Gardens on El Paseo.

## Casual & Contemporary Options

Birba is ideal for laid-back alfresco dining, serving craft cocktails, homemade pizza and pasta made with fresh ingredients sourced from Coachella Valley farms.

Trilussa is a lively restaurant in Cathedral City offering traditional Italian dishes like chicken piccata, veal cacciatore and lasagna Bolognese, with locals raving about their olive tapenade and a daily happy hour from 4-7 p.m.

Sammy G's in downtown Palm Springs merges traditional Italian cuisine with California flair, with their wild mushroom ravioli being a popular dish.

## Family-Friendly Classics

Mario's Italian Cafés has served the Coachella Valley for over 50 years with locations throughout the region, known for old-world family recipes and award-winning restaurants.

Mimmo's Italian Restaurant is voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant and Favorite Pizza.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Pinzimini in Rancho Mirage as a romantic date night destination:

## Location & Atmosphere

Pinzimini is nestled within the stunning Westin Mission Hills in Rancho Mirage and showcases Tuscan-style cuisine. You can dine indoors or enjoy al fresco dining on their pet-friendly patio, offering breathtaking views.

## Date Night Suitability

Pinzimini can be a pleasant date-night spot when you want a relaxed, hotel-restaurant vibe with an outdoor patio. Guests praise the pleasant outdoor seating, attentive servers (some named staff get repeat praise), and dishes like pasta, steak and sea bass. The outdoor dining is designed to maximize views, and for those on the perimeter (which most tables are), you are rewarded with an expansive view of the lush resort property.

## Cuisine & Menu

Under the expert guidance of Chef Shawn Aoki since 2012, guests can indulge in an array of delectable dishes ranging from antipastos to artisanal pizzas and paninis.

## Service & Dining Experience

Visitors report arriving for romantic dinners and having the experience blow them away, with staff being incredibly knowledgeable and attentive without being intrusive, and guidance through the menu being beyond helpful.

**Phone:** (760) 770-2150  
**Hours:** Monday-Sunday, 7:00 am - 10:00 pm

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chef Joel Delmond, his James Beard connection, and Pinzimini:

## Chef Joel Delmond

Chef Joel Delmond's impressive career has been a continuing compilation of education and experiences garnered from some of the finest chefs and restaurants throughout the world. He began his culinary studies in his native France at the Orleans Culinary School where he was honored as a 2nd apprentice. In Sologne, under the tutelage of the famed Pierre Mesple, he won a top regional cooking award for game and river fish.

## James Beard Recognition

"The biggest honor for Chef Delmond was to be a guest chef at the James Beard House in 2002." It's important to note that while Chef Joel Delmond is not a recipient of this award, he has cooked in the James Beard House, a sensational recognition of its own.

## Pinzimini Restaurant

Pinzimini is a Palm Springs restaurant featuring Italian inspired cuisine with Modern Tuscan style entrees as well as small plates with antipasti, pizza, pasta and paninis. Delmond has been with Westin Mission Hills for more than 25 years.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Pinzimini for breakfast and brunch in Rancho Mirage:

## Pinzimini Overview

Pinzimini is located within the Westin Mission Hills in Rancho Mirage and has been a contemporary dining gem since 2012 under Chef Shawn Aoki's guidance, specializing in Tuscan-style cuisine.

## Breakfast & Brunch Offerings

The restaurant offers a weekend breakfast buffet every Saturday and Sunday featuring pancakes, waffles, an omelet station, freshly baked pastries, and unlimited Bloody Marys or Mimosas. Guests can enjoy an array of dishes ranging from antipastos to artisanal pizzas and paninis, all crafted with care and precision.

## Dining Experience

The restaurant features coastal-inspired cuisine with fresh, seasonal ingredients and bold flavors, offering both indoor dining and al fresco dining on a pet-friendly patio with breathtaking views. 

Pinzimini offers excellent food and attentive service, with particularly good eggs Benedict for breakfast. Reviews highlight popular breakfast items like poached eggs with crispy hash browns and buttermilk pancakes.

You can order takeout by calling (760) 770-2150.

content.text:
I have enough information. Notable findings:
- The restaurant is at The Westin **Mission Hills** Golf Resort & Spa (not just "Westin Rancho Mirage" — both names appear; Marriott has rebranded portfolios). Address is 71333 Dinah Shore Dr, Rancho Mirage.
- Chef confusion: Multiple sources mention **Shawn Aoki** "since 2012" as the chef, while Joel Delmond is the Executive Chef. Delmond was a James Beard House **guest chef in 2002** — NOT a Beard award honoree/nominee. "James Beard Honored" is misleading framing if interpreted as an award.
- 256 Yelp reviews confirmed; 1,140 OpenTable confirmed (3.7 stars); TripAdvisor active with 376 reviews at 4.0.
- Site returns 403 to fetchers — confirmed.
- Does NOT surface in "best Italian Coachella Valley" top lists — confirmed (Il Corso, Mamma Gina, Birba, Trilussa, Sammy G's, Mario's, Mimmo's dominate).
- USA Today 10Best mention is indirect (Wanderlog aggregates from "reputable sites including USA TODAY 10BEST") — flagged.

confirms: Yelp shows 256 reviews (independently confirmed in Yelp listing title)
OpenTable shows 1,140 diner ratings at 3.7 stars (confirmed)
TripAdvisor listing is active (376 reviews, 4.0 rating per current search)
Opened ~2012 at the Westin (Foursquare reviews date back to December 2012; multiple sources cite 'since 2012')
Own site pinziminipalmsprings.com returns HTTP 403 to automated fetchers (independently verified via WebFetch)
Domain name 'pinziminipalmsprings.com' is geographically misleading (restaurant is at 71333 Dinah Shore Dr, Rancho Mirage)

Does NOT surface in 'best Italian Coachella Valley' top lists — independent searches return Il Corso, Mamma Gina, Birba, Trilussa, Sammy G's, Mario's, Mimmo's as the dominant names; Pinzimini absent from top-tier Italian lists
Surfaces in brunch/breakfast queries with weekend buffet mentions
Mostly resort-context visibility (Marriott, OpenTable, Westin pages) rather than cuisine-first visibility
Sentiment is mixed-to-positive: service consistently praised, food described as variable/uneven
contradicts: Claim: 'Executive Chef Joel Delmond referenced as James Beard Honored' — what's actually true: Delmond was a GUEST CHEF at the James Beard House in 2002. This is a venue invitation, not a James Beard Award or nomination. Framing him as 'Beard Honored' is misleading without that qualifier.

Claim: Resort is 'The Westin Rancho Mirage Golf Resort & Spa' — what's actually true: Multiple authoritative sources (Yelp, Foursquare, TripAdvisor user reviews, Wanderlog) refer to it as 'The Westin Mission Hills Golf Resort & Spa.' Marriott's own page uses 'Westin Rancho Mirage' branding, suggesting a recent rename. Brief should note the dual-naming issue, which itself is an agent-visibility hazard.

Implicit chef claim: Sources also widely credit 'Chef Shawn Aoki since 2012' as the operational chef — there appears to be a Shawn Aoki / Joel Delmond confusion across sources. Delmond is Executive Chef of the resort property; Aoki is repeatedly named as the restaurant chef. The brief should disambiguate.
flagged_as_unverified: USA Today 10Best listing — could not independently confirm a direct USA Today 10Best Rancho Mirage feature for Pinzimini. Wanderlog claims to aggregate 'including USA TODAY 10BEST' but no direct USA Today URL surfaced in searches.
Google review count (already flagged in claim)
Combined cross-platform ~1,500-2,000 figure is plausible (256 Yelp + 1,140 OpenTable + 376 TripAdvisor = 1,772) but excludes Google
Whether the 403 on the own site is bot-protection vs intentional block could not be distinguished without further probing
Visibility gap score of 6 — directionally consistent with findings but a subjective rubric value
input.notes: Brief is directionally correct on the central thesis: Pinzimini is a resort-anchored Italian restaurant that is invisible in cuisine-first regional queries and visible only in resort-context or brunch queries. Two material corrections needed: (1) the James Beard credential is overstated — Delmond was a James Beard House guest chef in 2002, which is a venue cooking invitation, not 'Beard Honored'/award status; (2) there is a chef-attribution inconsistency in source material between Joel Delmond (Executive Chef, property-wide) and Shawn Aoki (cited 'since 2012' for the restaurant) that the brief should disambiguate. The resort name itself is in flux between 'Westin Mission Hills' and 'Westin Rancho Mirage' — itself an agent-readability problem worth calling out. Aggregated review count math checks out at ~1,770 ex-Google, supporting the 1,500-2,000 range.
